2026-03-31.content:
Unified Maintenance supports
[viewing maintenance activities](https://docs.cloud.google.com/unified-maintenance/docs/view-maintenance-api)
for App Hub applications that you have created in
[App Hub](https://docs.cloud.google.com/app-hub/docs/overview) or
[Application Design Center](https://docs.cloud.google.com/application-design-center/docs/overview).

summary.title: Unified Maintenance for App Hub
summary.summary: Unified Maintenance now supports viewing maintenance activities for App Hub applications. This includes applications created in App Hub or Application Design Center. You can now easily track and manage maintenance schedules for your applications.

2026-02-27.content:
Unified Maintenance is generally available ([GA](https://docs.cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages)).

Use Unified Maintenance to gain better visibility into maintenance activities
impacting your Google Cloud resources.

Learn more in the [Unified Maintenance overview](https://docs.cloud.google.com/unified-maintenance/docs/overview).

summary.title: Unified Maintenance is now generally available
summary.summary: Unified Maintenance is now generally available, providing enhanced visibility into maintenance activities affecting your Google Cloud resources. Learn more about this new feature in the overview documentation.

2026-02-27.content:
Unified Maintenance displays maintenance activities for these Google Cloud
products:

* Cloud SQL - MySQL
* Cloud SQL - Postgres
* Cloud SQL - SQL Server
* AlloyDB for PostgreSQL
* Looker
* Compute Engine virtual machine (VM) instances or bare metal instances
* Google Kubernetes Engine
* Cloud Interconnect
* Cloud Composer

For more information, see [Supported services](https://docs.cloud.google.com/unified-maintenance/docs/supported-services).

summary.title: Unified Maintenance for Cloud Services
summary.summary: Unified Maintenance now provides a single view for maintenance activities across various Google Cloud services including databases, compute, and networking. This feature simplifies tracking and managing upcoming disruptions. You can find a full list of supported services in the documentation.

2026-02-27.content:
Unified Maintenance is available in the following regions:

* `asia-east1`
* `australia-southeast2`
* `europe-central2`
* `europe-west2`
* `europe-west9`
* `us-west4`
* `us-east5`

For more information, see [Unified Maintenance locations](https://docs.cloud.google.com/unified-maintenance/docs/locations).

summary.title: Unified Maintenance Now Live
summary.summary: Unified Maintenance is now available in a growing list of regions, including `asia-east1`, `australia-southeast2`, `europe-central2`, `europe-west2`, `europe-west9`, `us-west4`, and `us-east5`. For a full list of supported locations, please refer to the documentation.

2026-02-27.content:
Unified Maintenance displays Cloud Composer maintenance activity for resources:

* `cloud_composer_environment`

For more information about Cloud Composer resources displayed by
Unified Maintenance, see [Supported services](https://docs.cloud.google.com/unified-maintenance/docs/supported-services).

summary.title: Maintenance for Composer environments
summary.summary: Unified Maintenance now shows Cloud Composer environment maintenance. This feature provides visibility into planned activities for your Composer resources. For a full list of supported services, please refer to our documentation.
